title: Buttery Fluffy Filipino Rice Flour Pancakes
description: A crowd-pleasing Filipino dish featuring caramelized sugar sauce, these buttery and fluffy pancakes are a delight to eat. They are also Jain-friendly, making them accessible to a wide range of diets.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together rice flour, sugar, and salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk together water, melted butter, eggs, and vanilla extract.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and mix until smooth.
The batter should still be slightly lumpy.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat.
Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, scoop the batter onto the pan.
Cook for 2-3 minutes or until bubbles appear on the surface and the edges start to dry.
Flip the pancake and cook for another 1-2 minutes or until golden brown.
To make the caramel sauce, melt 1/2 cup sugar in a saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly.
Increase heat to medium and cook until the sugar turns golden brown.
Serve the pancakes with caramel sauce and enjoy!
